criteria_checked.criterion_ref: B1-closure — CI step 7 lexeme grep scoped to .claude/install + bin/lib/dmc-doctor.py, empty today (plan L376-382 vs M8 :507)
criteria_checked.note: Ran the Rev 2 step-7 grep AS WRITTEN over .claude/install + bin/lib/dmc-doctor.py redirect-free: exit 1, ZERO matches. The M8 :507 authoritative form also returns zero over the same two paths. Repo-wide the bypass lexeme is in 10 tracked files / 22 line-occurrences (.codex/config.toml, adapters/codex/README.md, docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md, evidence/plans) — a repo-wide grep WOULD be permanently red, so the two-path scoping is the correct and necessary fix. Blocker B1 is CLOSED. Two honesty/robustness notes folded to advisories (nc-lexeme rendering; the '~20 files' figure).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: A1-fold — green-path stages exactly scope.lock files[].path; G1+G2 pass; untracked run artifacts don't trip G3/G4/G5 (plan L254-263, L414, L442 vs gate-check-runner.sh)
criteria_checked.note: gate-check-runner.sh L39 defines staged := git diff --cached --name-only; G1 (staged ⊆ allowlist) and G2 (allowlist fully staged) both pass when staged == allowlist == scope.lock files[].path. G3 (L52-61) and G5 (L69-70) are staged-scoped, and G4 (L63-67) checks ONLY DEFAULT_PROTECTED paths via git status --porcelain — none touched by the E2E — so untracked .harness/runs/<rid>/ artifacts are invisible to every check. Precondition pinned in both fixtures + the schema. Residual (advisory): G2 tests a cached diff, so staged allowlist paths must be modified/new.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: A2-fold — chain sub-gate labeled ACCOUNTABILITY/PROVENANCE with the append-log-exemption disclosure in schema §2 (plan L324-331)
criteria_checked.note: Plan §2 explicitly labels the chain sub-gate ACCOUNTABILITY/PROVENANCE tier, discloses the run-dir append-log exemption (deleted chain + deleted authorization ⇒ PASS-with-note), and names diff-scope + Ring-1 postbash as the actual mutation-detection floor — no stronger claim.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: A3-fold — composer pinned to real filename bin/lib/dmc-v0.6.5-decision-trace.py (plan L277)
criteria_checked.note: bin/lib/dmc-v0.6.5-decision-trace.py exists (answer verb, exit 0/1/2, Q1-Q6 emitter); dmc-v0.6.5-decision-traceability.* does NOT exist. The prose alias is disclosed as the tool-family name, not the path.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: A4-fold — fixtures pinned to five-type-set --evidence-type + resolvable --verification-ref in BOTH fixtures (plan L408-411, L442)
criteria_checked.note: dmc-evidence-ledger.py mint accepts --evidence-type and --verification-ref; EVIDENCE_TYPES byte-identical to v0.6.2's set. Plan uses verification-report/test-result (both members). Both fixture descriptions pin the requirement, so the v0.6.2 receipts sub-gate passes by construction.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: plan-instance validity + composed-tool CLI fidelity (full re-review)
criteria_checked.note: bin/dmc validate plan ⇒ VALID (exit 0). Every verb the composer invokes exists with the cited semantics: scope-lock --adjudicate (0/3), v0.6.2 validate (0/1/2), v0.6.3 gate, v0.6.4 trace/authorize, v0.6.5 answer, evidence-ledger coverage/mint/--validate-ledger, approvals --validate (0/3) with POST_VERIFICATION_KINDS={release,push,waiver}, dmc delegation check --run, dmc worker apply-check, dmc validate verification (0/3). No contract mismatch introduced by the amendments.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: scope/allow-row completeness + single-owner DAG + M10 boundary (full re-review)
criteria_checked.note: Every mutated path has an allow-row incl. the contract-class delegation.schema.md (explicitly authorized) and the greenfield .github/ surface (master row L82). Single-owner rule holds; blockedBy forms a proper DAG. M10 boundary respected — DMC_V1_RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md is an optional extension point. Honest-scope wording intact (PARTIAL never PASS; FLAG ≠ fail; committed-diff blindness + --base disclosed; CI-green explicitly post-push).

advisories.statement: nc-lexeme fidelity: step 7's regex as written (plan L376) uses `nc ` (nc + literal space) and the Rev 2 header (L27) uses bare `nc`, but the cited M8 :507 AUTHORITATIVE pattern is `\b(nc|netcat)\b` (word-bounded). Both forms return ZERO over the two scoped paths TODAY (verified), so CI is green now and the B1 closure stands — but `nc ` is false-positive-prone: any future line containing 'sync ', 'async ', 'func ', 'since ' would match and turn CI red on a benign token. Recommend pinning the byte-exact M8 :507 pattern `\b(nc|netcat)\b|curl|wget|urllib|http\.client|socket|requests|smtplib|ftplib|api\.|CODEX_API_KEY|GLM_API_KEY|dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` in the dmc-ci.yml step so the 'FULL M8 set' claim is literally true and the word-boundary guards against false positives.

Honesty imprecision (non-load-bearing): the plan states the bypass lexeme 'legitimately appears in ~20 tracked files' (L23, L380). Empirically it is 10 tracked files / 22 line-occurrences (git grep). The B1 closure is unaffected — 10 > 0 still proves a repo-wide grep would be red — but the figure should read '10 files (22 occurrences)' for accuracy.

A1 residual (fixture-authoring): G2 ('allowlist fully staged') tests membership in git diff --cached --name-only, i.e. a cached DIFF, not the index. git add of an unmodified tracked allowlist path produces no cached diff and thus FAILs G2. The Rev 2 precondition is sufficient only if every scope.lock files[].path is modified or new in the run. The E2E performs 'one benign in-scope edit' — if scope.lock files[] contains more than that one path, the unmodified members break G2. Pin in the fixture that scope.lock files[] must equal the modified/new set (the all-9-PASS acceptance row will catch a violation loudly).
